page: Google Cloud Platform c4n-standard-48 is a compute-focused virtual machine instance belonging to the c4n server family. It is configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture. The system features 180.0 GB of system memory, which yields a resource density of 3.75 GB of RAM per virtual core.

This instance configuration does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring external network configuration or NAT gateways for public internet connectivity.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation and the 1:3.75 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the c4n-standard-48 is designed for compute-intensive workloads that require predictable CPU performance without the overhead of shared resources. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for high-throughput processing, batch computing, and web serving workloads that do not require local SSD storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4n-standard-48 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance within the c4n family, featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 180.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a resource density of 3.75 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage for data persistence. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. With its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced compute-to-memory ratio, the c4n-standard-48 is engineered for predictable, high-compute workloads. It is well-suited for applications such as batch processing, web serving, and distributed compute tasks that require consistent CPU performance without the need for specialized hardware accelerators or local physical disks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4n-standard-48 is a dedicated x86_64 server featuring 48 vCPUs and 180 GB RAM. It lacks local storage and GPUs, making it ideal for network-reliant compute workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4n-standard-48 is a dedicated x86_64 instance with 48 vCPUs and 180 GB RAM, designed for compute-intensive workloads.
tagline: A dedicated forty-eight core x86_64 compute instance with one hundred and eighty gigabytes of memory for predictable, non-accelerated workloads.
bullet_points: 180.0 GB of system memory providing 3.75 GB per core
No local storage or integrated GPU hardware included
Zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses allocated
Optimized for compute-heavy workloads and batch processing
